data.summary: The Department of Veterans Affairs Office of Information and Technology (OIT) is conducting market research for a Next-Generation EHR Interoperability Gateway solution. Despite using formal Section L/M headers typically associated with solicitations, this document explicitly and repeatedly states it is NOT a solicitation and no contract will be awarded. No proposals are being requested. No funding has been allocated -- a future solicitation is contingent on budget approval in FY2027. VA is seeking capability statements from industry to inform acquisition strategy for a potential future procurement. The desired solution must support HL7 FHIR R4 standards, enable real-time clinical data exchange, and integrate with VA's Cerner Millennium EHR (Oracle Health) and legacy VistA systems at approximately 40 facilities.
data.periodOfPerformance: Not specified (Sources Sought -- no solicitation issued)
data.agency: Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology (OIT)
data.title: Next-Generation Electronic Health Record (EHR) Interoperability Gateway

deadlines.label: Information Response Requested By (Non-binding)
deadlines.notes: This is NOT a proposal submission deadline. This is a non-binding date for informational responses to a Sources Sought notice. Late responses will still be reviewed. No contract will be awarded from this notice.
deadlines.rawText: Information responses are requested by April 15, 2026, but late responses will still be reviewed.

warnings: THIS IS NOT A SOLICITATION. No proposals are being requested. The April 15, 2026 date is a non-binding request for information responses.
Despite Section L and Section M headers, this document explicitly states it is a Sources Sought notice only.
No funding has been allocated. A future solicitation is contingent on FY2027 budget approval.

data.overview: VA OIT is seeking market research information on EHR Interoperability Gateway solutions. Desired capabilities include FHIR R4 compliant API gateway with SMART on FHIR authorization, message transformation between HL7 v2/CDA/FHIR formats, patient matching and identity resolution, audit logging and consent management per 38 CFR Part 1, 99.99% uptime SLA, FedRAMP High authorization, and scalability to 50M+ transactions per day. Integration required with VA's Cerner Millennium EHR (Oracle Health) and legacy VistA systems at approximately 40 facilities. NOTE: Section M explicitly states responses will NOT be evaluated for award purposes -- no scores or ratings will be assigned.

requirements.requirement: FHIR R4 compliant API gateway with support for SMART on FHIR authorization
Message transformation between HL7 v2, CDA, and FHIR formats
Patient matching and identity resolution across disparate systems
Audit logging and consent management per 38 CFR Part 1
Scalability to handle 50 million+ transactions per day

requiredDocuments.name: Capability Statement
requiredDocuments.description: Company overview, size standard, socioeconomic status, relevant EHR interoperability experience, technical approach overview, ROM timeline, and potential teaming arrangements. Limited to 15 pages.

contacts.name: VA OIT Market Research
contacts.notes: No individual contact name provided. All correspondence directed to this email. Subject line must reference: SS-0234 EHR Interoperability Gateway - [Company Name].
contacts.organization: Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology

redFlags.whyItMatters: Despite using formal Section L/M headers that suggest a solicitation, this document explicitly and repeatedly states: 'THIS IS NOT A SOLICITATION FOR PROPOSALS AND NO CONTRACT WILL BE AWARDED FROM THIS NOTICE.' The Section L/M format is misleading but the content is unambiguous.
redFlags.mitigation: Treat this as market research only. Do not invest significant B&P resources. Submit a brief capability statement if interested in positioning for a potential future solicitation.
redFlags.flag: Sources Sought with misleading Section L/M headers -- NOT a solicitation

redFlags.whyItMatters: No funding has been allocated for this requirement. A future solicitation is explicitly stated as contingent on budget approval in FY2027. There is no guarantee a solicitation will ever be issued.
redFlags.mitigation: Monitor VA OIT procurement activity and FY2027 budget developments. Keep response effort minimal and proportional to the speculative nature.
redFlags.flag: No funding allocated -- future solicitation contingent on FY2027 budget approval

risks.whyItMatters: The scope is extremely large (171 VAMCs, 1,100+ outpatient facilities, 9 million enrolled veterans, 50M+ transactions/day) and involves complex integration with both Oracle Health/Cerner and legacy VistA systems. If a future solicitation is issued, this would be a very large and complex contract.
risks.mitigation: Begin early assessment of EHR interoperability capabilities and potential teaming arrangements if interested in a future solicitation.
risks.flag: Extremely large and complex scope if future solicitation is issued

incumbentInfo.notes: No incumbent identified. This is a Sources Sought for market research on a potential future requirement. No existing contract exists for this specific interoperability gateway.

recommendations: This is a Sources Sought notice -- no past performance evaluation will occur. If a future solicitation is issued, prepare references for EHR interoperability, HL7 FHIR implementations, and large-scale healthcare IT integrations.

data.narrativeSummary: No past performance evaluation applicable. This is a Sources Sought notice for market research purposes only. Section M explicitly states responses will NOT be evaluated for award purposes.

data.confidenceExplanation: High confidence in NO_GO decision. Despite formal Section L/M headers, the document explicitly and repeatedly states this is NOT a solicitation. No contract will be awarded. No funding has been allocated. No evaluation factors apply (Section M states responses will NOT be evaluated for award). A future solicitation depends on FY2027 budget approval. There is nothing to bid on.
requiredActions: NO BID: This is a Sources Sought notice, not a solicitation -- there is nothing to bid on
Consider submitting a brief capability statement (15 pages or less) to register interest with VA OIT if EHR interoperability is a strategic capability area
Monitor VA OIT procurement activity and FY2027 budget decisions for a potential future solicitation
Assess internal EHR interoperability capabilities and potential teaming arrangements for future positioning

criteria.rationale: No incumbent identified for this specific requirement. However, large health IT vendors with existing VA relationships may have advantages if a future solicitation is issued.

data.summaryJustification: NO_GO. Despite Section L/M headers that mimic a formal solicitation, this is explicitly a Sources Sought notice. No contract will be awarded. No proposals are being requested. No funding has been allocated. No evaluation factors apply. A future solicitation depends on FY2027 budget approval. There is nothing to bid on. Consider submitting a brief capability statement for positioning purposes only.
